stem: You use a circuit-library state preparation routine that outputs a specific n-qubit state $|ψ⟩$. How is this typically integrated into a larger circuit?

options.A: You must manually copy all of its gates into your circuit definition
options.B: You can append it as a subcircuit or instruction at the beginning of your main circuit
options.C: You must convert it to OpenQASM text and paste it into your code
options.D: It can only be used as a standalone circuit and not combined

stem: You have a parameterized circuit and want to visualize how the parameters appear in the diagram. What behavior should you expect from qc.draw("mpl")?

options.A: Parameters will be hidden; only gate types will be shown
options.B: The diagram will show symbolic parameter names (like $θ_0$, $θ_1$) as labels on the gates
options.C: The drawer will fail unless all parameters are numerically bound
options.D: All parameters will be treated as 0 during drawing

stem: You are building a variational algorithm with layers of parameterized Ry and CNOT gates. Which practice best leverages the circuit library and parameterization features?

options.A: Transpile the circuit first and then try to introduce parameters into the transpiled output
options.B: Manually code every Ry and CNOT gate for each layer in plain QuantumCircuit
options.C: Use a TwoLocal or EfficientSU2-style template from the circuit library with symbolic parameters
options.D: Avoid symbolic parameters and bake fixed angles into the circuit

stem: In Qiskit visualization, what is a typical use of a style dictionary when drawing a circuit with the Matplotlib-based drawer?

options.A: To control colors, fonts, and gate shapes in the circuit diagram
options.B: To define parameter values for parameterized gates
options.C: To specify initial qubit states $|0⟩$ or $|1⟩$
options.D: To change backend-specific transpilation settings

stem: You want to compare two Qiskit circuits to check if they implement the same unitary (up to global phase). Which workflow is most appropriate?

options.A: Compare their text diagrams produced by qc.draw("text")
options.B: Check that they have the same number of gates and qubits
options.C: Convert both to Operator objects and compare the matrices
options.D: Run both on a simulator for a few random input states and compare outputs

stem: Which best describes the relationship between QuantumCircuit and Operator in Qiskit?

options.A: Operators can only be created from measurement results, not from circuits
options.B: QuantumCircuit and Operator are unrelated; they live in different namespaces
options.C: QuantumCircuit is a subclass of Operator
options.D: Operator provides a mathematical representation of the linear map implemented by a circuit

stem: You are working with Qiskit operators and want to represent the Hamiltonian $H=0.5Z_0Z_1$+$X_1$ for use with primitives. What is the most appropriate Qiskit class to represent this operator compactly?

options.A: DensityMatrix from qiskit.quantum_info
options.B: Operator from qiskit.quantum_info with a full 4×4 matrix
options.C: PauliList from qiskit.quantum_info without coefficients
options.D: SparsePauliOp from qiskit.quantum_info built from Pauli strings and coefficients

stem: You create a 3-qubit circuit and want to visualize the statevector after applying some gates using the state_city visualization. Which Qiskit toolchain correctly produces this visualization?

options.A: Simulate the circuit with AerSimulator(method='statevector'), get the final statevector, and pass it to plot_state_city
options.B: Use Statevector.from_int only, because visualizations are created automatically when constructing the object
options.C: Call circuit.draw('mpl') and interpret the diagram as a state-city plot
options.D: Use the Sampler primitive and directly pass its quasi-distribution to plot_state_city

stem: You need to visualize how the layout and gate structure of a circuit changes after transpilation at a certain optimization level. Which pair of visualizations best helps you compare pre- and post-transpilation circuits on a specific backend?

options.A: Use plot_state_city before and after transpilation to compare amplitude distributions
options.B: Use plot_histogram on simulated results of both circuits to compare measurement distributions
options.C: Use plot_error_map for the backend and plot_histogram of circuit counts
options.D: Use circuit.draw('mpl') before and circuit.draw('mpl') after calling transpile, with the same reverse_bits and fold settings

stem: You are optimizing a circuit for a specific hardware backend with limited connectivity. Which transpiler pass or configuration most directly addresses qubit routing to satisfy the coupling map?

options.A: Increasing optimization_level in transpile to 3
options.B: Using layout and routing passes, such as SetLayout and StochasticSwap, in a custom PassManager
options.C: Changing the basis_gates to match the backend's native gate set
options.D: Using RemoveBarriers to minimize unnecessary barriers

stem: You submit multiple jobs to a runtime session to run parameter sweeps using the Sampler primitive. You want to track all jobs and handle failures. Which Qiskit pattern best supports job management in this context?

options.A: Avoid tracking jobs because runtime guarantees completion for all submitted tasks
options.B: Use a runtime Session with Sampler, collecting the returned job handles, and periodically query their statuses via job.status()
options.C: Use only blocking calls like result = sampler.run(...).result() and ignore job handles
options.D: Use backend.run for all tasks and manually store job IDs in a list for later inspection

stem: You are designing a parameterized circuit using Qiskit and want to express it in OpenQASM 3 syntax, including classical control based on measurement outcomes. Which Qiskit component most directly helps generate OpenQASM 3 code from your circuit?

options.A: qiskit.qasm3 exporter to convert a QuantumCircuit with classical control into OpenQASM 3
options.B: qiskit.qasm2 exporter to produce OpenQASM 2 code, then manually edit it to OpenQASM 3
options.C: Only the QuantumCircuit.draw() method, which can output OpenQASM 3 when using the 'qasm3' style
options.D: Qiskit does not yet provide any support for OpenQASM 3 export

options.A: Reset the qubit, apply $R_x(π/2)$, measure, and conditionally apply $Z$ gate only when the outcome is 1
options.B: Apply $R_x(π/2)$ measure, and then conditionally reset based on the classical bit
options.C: Apply an $R_x(π/2)$ rotation, always apply a $Z$ gate, then measure into a classical bit
options.D: Prepare $|1⟩$, measure, then always apply $Z$ gate after measurement

stem: You are using Qiskit’s Sampler primitive to estimate outcome probabilities for a list of circuits. Which statement best describes how the primitive reports these probabilities and how they differ from raw counts?

options.A: Sampler returns raw counts per bitstring with no normalization
options.B: Sampler returns only expectation values of Pauli observables
options.C: Sampler returns quasi-probabilities per bitstring that may not be exactly non-negative or normalized
options.D: Sampler returns exact probabilities with infinite precision, independent of the number of shots

stem: You have a QuantumCircuit with custom Gate and Instruction objects and want to run it on a real device that only supports a limited set of basis gates. What is the main role of the transpiler in this context?

options.A: It only optimizes the visual layout of the circuit diagram
options.B: It decomposes high-level and custom gates into the device's basis gates while respecting connectivity and constraints
options.C: It automatically chooses the best quantum algorithm for you
options.D: It replaces all two-qubit gates with single-qubit gates to reduce error

stem: You are using the IBM Quantum runtime to run an iterative algorithm with many calls to Estimator. What is the main advantage of using a single long-lived Session for these calls instead of creating a new session each time?

options.A: Multiple sessions are always faster because they parallelize across different backends
options.B: A single session guarantees zero queue time for all jobs
options.C: Sessions only affect billing, not technical behavior
options.D: A single session groups related jobs, reduces overhead, and can provide priority handling compared to many short sessions

stem: You are reviewing a transpiled circuit and see many inserted swap gates between certain qubits. What is the primary reason the transpiler added these swap gates?

options.A: To change the measurement basis of the final measurements
options.B: To increase the circuit depth for benchmarking purposes
options.C: To satisfy the device’s coupling map by moving logical qubits so that two-qubit gates act only on connected qubit pairs
options.D: To reduce the overall number of two-qubit gates by replacing them with swaps

stem: You are using Qiskit’s qasm3 exporter on a circuit that includes mid-circuit measurements and classical conditions. How does this relate to the notion of dynamic circuits in Qiskit and OpenQASM 3?

options.A: OpenQASM 3 only supports static circuits without measurements
options.B: Dynamic circuits are not supported in OpenQASM 3, so the exporter will fail
options.C: The exporter can express many dynamic-circuit features, such as mid-circuit measurement and classical branching, as OpenQASM 3 control constructs
options.D: Dynamic circuits in Qiskit are fundamentally incompatible with any text-based representation

stem: You are building an intermediate-level Qiskit application and want to structure your workflow using primitives and the runtime. Which of the following is a recommended design pattern?

options.A: Use primitives like Sampler and Estimator inside a runtime Session to implement algorithm-specific logic on the client side
options.B: Use backend.run for everything and ignore primitives, since they add unnecessary complexity
options.C: Implement your entire algorithm as a monolithic runtime program to avoid client-side iteration
options.D: Avoid sessions and submit each primitive call as an isolated job to maximize flexibility

stem: You are translating a simple Qiskit circuit into OpenQASM 3 by hand and want to ensure that it matches Qiskit's qubit and classical bit indexing conventions. Which principle should you follow?

options.A: Always reverse the qubit order in OpenQASM 3 because Qiskit uses the opposite convention
options.B: Ensure that qubits and bits are declared in OpenQASM 3 arrays with consistent indexing starting at 0, and map Qiskit’s register and bit ordering directly to these indices
options.C: Ignore indexing details because simulators automatically correct ordering mismatches
options.D: Use 1-based indexing in OpenQASM 3 for both qubits and bits

stem: In Qiskit which description best characterizes an "execution mode" for running circuits on backends?

options.A: It is a hardware parameter describing qubit coherence time
options.B: It refers to how jobs are scheduled and run, such as synchronous versus asynchronous interactions with the backend
options.C: It is a setting that adjusts optimization level during transpilation
options.D: It defines the measurement basis used for all qubits in a circuit

stem: After submitting a circuit to run on a hardware backend in Qiskit you receive a job object. Which method or approach best allows you to monitor the job’s progress until completion?

options.A: Resubmit the same job repeatedly until results appear
options.B: Ignore the job object and wait for an email notification from the provider
options.C: Directly access the backend’s internal scheduler logs
options.D: Use job status methods or a job monitor utility to poll the job’s state

stem: You submitted a long-running experiment to a hardware backend in Qiskit but then realize a bug in your circuit. How can you responsibly stop the experiment to avoid wasting hardware time?

options.A: Manually power down the backend if you have access
options.B: Submit an empty job to overwrite the old one
options.C: Use the job object’s cancel method (or equivalent) to request job cancellation
options.D: Close your Python session and hope the job stops automatically

stem: In Qiskit which practice is most appropriate for saving a job so that you can retrieve its results later, even from a different session or machine?

options.A: Store the job’s unique job ID or use built-in job history mechanisms provided by the account or provider
options.B: Copy the circuit’s source code; the provider will automatically find the matching job
options.C: Screenshot the output of the run call and reconstruct results manually
options.D: Memorize the job’s status message, which is enough to reload it later

stem: In a dynamic circuit, what is the best description of classical feedforward?

options.A: A measurement result is used to decide later operations.
options.B: A gate is automatically decomposed into single-qubit gates.
options.C: The transpiler chooses the backend based on the measurement result.
options.D: The circuit is converted to a density matrix.
